Step:4 Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Standing Military Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it down to shoulder level, with your palms facing forward.
Step:4 Press the barbell upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to shoulder level.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Wide Hand Push Up
instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the trap bar on the ground in front of you.
Step:2 Bend at the hips and knees to lower yourself down and grip the handles of the trap bar with an overhand grip.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and chest up as you begin to lift the trap bar off the ground by extending your hips and knees.
Step:4 As you lift, focus on driving through your heels and squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Lower the trap bar back down to the ground by bending at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight throughout the movement.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Stiff Leg Good Morning
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, hinge forward at the hips, pushing your glutes back.
Step:4 Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:5 Engage your glutes and hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Weighted Muscle Up
instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Step:3 As you reach the top of the movement, transition your grip so that your palms are facing towards you.
Step:4 Continue pulling yourself up until your chest reaches the bar, then pause for a moment.
Step:5 Slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: EZ Barbell Seated Curls
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the ez barbell to hang down in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the ez barbell upwards towards your shoulders.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly lower the ez barbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell One Arm Prone Curl
instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down.
Step:2 Extend your arm fully, letting it hang straight down towards the floor.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arm stationary, curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: Dumbbell Incline Shrug
instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Place your feet flat on the ground and let your arms hang straight down with your palms facing your body.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, shrug your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Seated Bent Arm Lateral Raise
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Keeping your elbows bent, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Weighted Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold a weight in front of your chest or on your shoulders.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your hips down and back, as if sitting into a chair.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Push through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Weighted Triceps Dip on High Parallel Bars
instructions: Step:1 Position yourself between two parallel bars with your hands gripping the bars and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Bend your elbows and lower your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Step:2 Place a barbell on the ground in front of you, centered between your feet.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, to grip the barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:4 Engage your core and drive through your heels to lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Step:5 As you lift, keep your chest up and back straight, and push your hips forward to fully engage your glutes.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Assisted Pull-Up
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height settings.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang with your arms fully extended and your feet off the ground.
Step:4 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the handles, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the handles.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Kettlebell Bent Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in your right hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and press the kettlebell overhead with your right arm, keeping your elbow locked.
Step:3 Rotate your torso to the left, shifting your weight onto your left foot.
Step:4 Bend your left knee and lower your torso towards the ground, keeping your right arm extended overhead.
Step:5 As you lower, keep your eyes on the kettlebell and your chest lifted.
Step:6 Once your left hand touches the ground, push through your left foot and straighten your left leg, driving your hips forward.
Step:7 As you drive your hips forward, use your core and right arm to press the kettlebell back up to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

name: Cable Standing Hip Extension
instructions: Step:1 Attach a cable to a low pulley and stand facing away from the machine.
Step:2 Place the cable around your ankle and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the exercise.
Step:4 Slowly extend your leg straight back, squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:7 Switch sides and repeat with the other leg.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing your body. Perform with hold intensity.
Step:3 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Let your arms hang straight down, fully extended, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:5 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Seated Overhead Press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:4 Press the barbell overhead by extending your arms fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to shoulder level.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Standing Wide Military Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Step:2 Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Step:3 Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back to shoulder height and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:3 Pull your body up towards the bar by bending your elbows and bringing your chest towards the bar.
Step:4 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Lat Pulldown Full Range of Motion
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the lat pulldown machine with your knees positioned under the pads.
Step:2 Grasp the cable bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lean back slightly and keep your chest up, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Step:4 Pull the bar down towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Kettlebell Hang Clean
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your thighs.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Lower the kettlebell towards the ground, allowing it to swing between your legs.
Step:4 Quickly extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height.
Step:5 As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebell in the rack position, with your elbow tucked in and the kettlebell resting on your forearm.
Step:6 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position between your legs, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell One Arm Prone Hammer Curl
instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing your body and arm fully extended.
Step:2 Keep your upper arm stationary and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder, contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: Jump Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 Jump explosively off the ground, extending your hips, knees, and ankles.
Step:4 While in mid-air, quickly bring your arms forward for balance.
Step:5 Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately go into the next repetition.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Decline Close Grip to Skull Press
instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and hold a barbell with a close grip.
Step:2 Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms to press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Raise the barbell out to the sides, keeping your arms straight, until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Chest Dip on Straight Bar
instructions: Step:1 Grab the parallel bars with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Bend your knees and cross your ankles.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your arms until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Zercher Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the bar for stability.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Step:4 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Reverse Wrist Curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Lever Bent-Over Row
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Pull the v-bar towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the weight back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Standing Reverse Grip Curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps, bringing the barbell as close to your shoulders as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Weighted Standing Curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and exhale as you curl the weights up to shoulder level.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Lever Assisted Chin-Up
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the leverage machine to your desired resistance level.
Step:2 Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang with your arms fully extended, keeping your body straight.
Step:4 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the handles, leading with your chest.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the handles.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Jackknife Sit-Up
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms overhead.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your legs and upper body simultaneously, reaching your hands towards your toes.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs and upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Rope Extension Incline Bench Row
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable machine to the low pulley.
Step:2 Attach a rope handle to the cable machine and sit on the incline bench facing the machine.
Step:3 Grab the rope handle with an overhand grip and lean forward, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully, pulling the rope towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Bench Squat
instructions: Step:1 Place a dumbbell on the ground in front of a bench.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at the knees and hips to lower yourself down towards the bench, keeping your chest up and back straight.
Step:4 Once your glutes touch the bench, push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Lying Triceps Extension
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells down towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Weighted Bench Dip
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Single Arm Push-Up
instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and one hand placed slightly wider than the other.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 As you lower yourself, shift your weight to one side and lift the opposite arm off the ground, extending it straight out to the side.
Step:4 Push through your chest and triceps to raise your body back up to the starting position, while simultaneously lowering your extended arm back to the ground.
Step:5 Repeat the movement, alternating the arm you extend with each repetition.

name: Inverted Row on Bench
instructions: Step:1 Set up a bench at a height that allows your body to hang freely underneath it.
Step:2 Lie face up on the ground with your head towards the bench.
Step:3 Reach up and grab the bench with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Position your body so that your heels are on the ground and your arms are fully extended.
Step:5 Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull your chest up towards the bench.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Standing Wide-Grip Curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palms facing away from your body.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell upward by contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl Reverse
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down and arms fully extended.
Step:2 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Rear Delt Row
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your core engaged as you pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Seated Chest Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and cable handles to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip at shoulder height.
Step:4 Push the handles forward and away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed firmly against the bench.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and lift them to shoulder height.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:5 Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Full Squat
instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the barbell at shoulder height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:3 Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting on your traps.
Step:4 Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Unrack the bar and take a step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Step:6 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:7 Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as your flexibility allows.
Step:8 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body, arms extended downwards.
Step:3 Bend at your hips and knees, lowering the dumbbells towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Push through your heels and extend your hips and knees, lifting the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Wide-Grip Chest Dip on High Parallel Bars
instructions: Step:1 Position yourself on the parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body suspended in the air.
Step:2 Lean forward slightly and lower your body by bending your elbows until your chest is just above the bars.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell One Arm Bench Fly
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on top of your thigh.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbell pressed against your thigh.
Step:3 Using your free hand, help lift the dumbbell up to the starting position.
Step:4 Hold the dumbbell directly above your shoulder with your arm extended and palm facing inward.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Step:6 Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

name: Traditional Barbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at the hips, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping it close to your body.
Step:5 Feel the stretch in your hamstrings as you lower the barbell.
Step:6 Once you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, push your hips forward and stand up straight.
Step:7 Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:8 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Focus on traditional movement.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Preacher Curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a preacher bench with your upper arms resting on the pad and your chest against the support.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells as you contract your biceps.
Step:4 Continue to curl the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:6 Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Seated Biceps Curl To Shoulder Press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Step:4 Once your forearms are vertical, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Step:5 Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Medicine Ball Overhead Slam
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands above your head.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and forcefully slam the medicine ball down to the ground in front of you.
Step:4 As you slam the ball down, use your entire body to generate power, including your shoulders and core.
Step:5 Catch the ball on the bounce and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Lever One Arm Lateral High Row
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with one hand and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Pull the handle towards your body, keeping your elbow close to your side.
Step:4 Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Run
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your upper body relaxed.
Step:3 Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees up towards your chest and landing softly on the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Maintain a steady pace and continue jogging for the desired duration or distance.
Step:5 Remember to breathe deeply and maintain good posture throughout the exercise.

name: Cable Pull Through
instructions: Step:1 Stand facing away from the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grab the rope attachment with both hands and step forward, creating tension in the cable.
Step:3 Bend at the hips and lower your upper body until it is parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Engage your glutes and hamstrings to pull your body back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Intense Barbell Straight Leg Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at your hips and lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower the barbell until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Maintain intense form throughout.
Step:5 Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Straight Leg Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells towards the ground, allowing your torso to lean forward.
Step:3 Continue lowering the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your torso back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Cross-Over Lateral Pulldown
instructions: Step:1 Attach a cable handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height.
Step:2 Stand in the middle of the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step back to create tension in the cables.
Step:4 Lean forward slightly from the hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:5 Pull the handles down and across your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Kettlebell Double Windmill
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in your right hand.
Step:2 Extend your right arm overhead, keeping your eyes on the kettlebell.
Step:3 Rotate your left foot 45 degrees to the left and your right foot 90 degrees to the right.
Step:4 Bend at the waist to the left, keeping your right arm extended overhead.
Step:5 Lower the kettlebell towards the ground, reaching towards your left foot.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then engage your core and push through your right foot to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

name: Cable Preacher Curl
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine so that the preacher curl pad is at chest height.
Step:2 Sit on the preacher curl bench and place your upper arms on the pad, gripping the cable attachment with an underhand grip.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your elbows tucked in at your sides.
Step:4 Slowly curl the cable attachment up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the cable attachment back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Standing Cross-Over High Reverse Fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach a D-handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height.
Step:2 Stand in the middle of the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides, palms facing forward.
Step:4 Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a straight back throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Engage your shoulder muscles and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles towards the front of your body.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Spider Crawl Push Up
instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Bring your right knee towards your right elbow, keeping it off the ground.
Step:3 As you bring your right knee back, simultaneously lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Push back up to the starting position and repeat with your left knee towards your left elbow.
Step:5 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Assisted Chest Dip (Kneeling)
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell One Arm Incline Chest Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip, resting it on your shoulder.
Step:4 Push the dumbbell up and away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

name: Dumbbell Incline Hammer Press
instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench and position the dumbbells at shoulder level with your palms facing each other.
Step:3 Press the dumbbells up and away from your body until your arms are fully extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Neutral Grip Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Supine Reverse Fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and lie face down on a flat bench.
Step:2 Grasp the D-handle with each hand, palms facing down, and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, raise them out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Standing Fly
instructions: Step:1 Attach the handles to the cables at chest height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing away from the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Step:4 Step forward slightly to create tension in the cables.
Step:5 Keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the exercise.
Step:6 With a slight bend in your elbows, slowly bring your arms forward and together in front of your chest.
Step:7 Squeeze your chest muscles at the peak of the movement.
Step:8 Slowly reverse the movement, returning your arms to the starting position.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Push-Up Plus
instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Step:2 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:3 Once your chest is just above the ground, push through your hands to extend your arms and lift your upper body up.
Step:4 At the top of the movement, protract your shoulder blades by pushing your upper back towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement by retracting your shoulder blades and lowering your body back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Medicine Ball Chest Push With Run Release
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball at chest level.
Step:2 Step forward with your right foot and simultaneously push the medicine ball forward, extending your arms fully.
Step:3 As you push the ball forward, release it and let it roll forward.
Step:4 Quickly run forward and catch the ball before it hits the ground.
Step:5 Once you catch the ball, bring it back to your chest and repeat the movement with your left foot forward.
Step:6 Continue alternating legs and repeating the exercise for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Lateral Lunge
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Step:3 Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your left leg straight.
Step:4 Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side, stepping with your left foot.

name: Drop Push Up
instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Once your chest is just above the ground, quickly drop your knees to the ground.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith One Arm Row
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to waist level.
Step:2 Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with one hand using an overhand grip, with your palm facing down.
Step:5 Keep your elbow close to your body and pull the bar towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

name: Lever Bicep Curl
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and keep your elbows close to your sides.
Step:3 Exhale and curl the handles upward, contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Sled Hack Squat
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable position for your height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Step:3 Hold onto the handles or bars for stability.
Step:4 Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:5 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to raise your body back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Seated Wide-Grip Row
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Step:4 Pull the handle towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction.
Step:6 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Pulldown (Pro Lat Bar)
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that your thighs are parallel to the ground and your feet are flat on the floor.
Step:2 Grasp the lat bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit down and lean back slightly, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Step:4 Pull the bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Cuban Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Keeping your core engaged and your elbows slightly bent, press the dumbbells up and overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Step:3 Rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your wrists back to the starting position as you do so.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Glute Bridge
instructions: Step:1 Start by lying flat on your back on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Step:2 Place a barbell across your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and core muscles, then lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Step:5 Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Decline Sit-Up
instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your knees bent.
Step:2 Place your hands behind your head or across your chest.
Step:3 Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the bench, curling forward towards your knees.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Machine Reverse Decline Close Grip Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the smith machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Wide Grip Pull-Up
instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:3 Pull your body up towards the bar until your chin is above the bar.
Step:4 Lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable One Arm Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attachment at chest height.
Step:2 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and step back to create tension in the cable.
Step:3 Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Start with your arm fully extended and perpendicular to the floor.
Step:6 Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and push the bar down until your arm is fully extended.
Step:7 Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: Dumbbell Seated Alternate Press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:3 Press one dumbbell up overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbell back down to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:6 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Wide-Grip Upright Row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Low Bar Squat
instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the barbell at a height that allows you to comfortably rest it on your upper back.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned outwards.
Step:3 Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:4 Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Unrack the bar by straightening your legs and stepping back from the rack.
Step:6 Take a deep breath and brace your core.
Step:7 Initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:8 Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:9 Keep your chest up and your back straight throughout the movement.
Step:10 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:11 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells towards the ground, allowing your knees to bend slightly.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then push through your heels and engage your glutes to return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Lunge
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your chest up as you lower your body.
Step:4 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg.
Step:6 Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Close-Grip Press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Step:3 Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
Step:4 As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
Step:5 As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Seated One Arm Concentration Curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a cable handle with one hand and place your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Step:3 Keep your upper arm stationary and curl the cable handle towards your shoulder while exhaling.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position while inhaling.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: Dumbbell Plyo Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 As you reach the bottom of the squat, explode upward, jumping off the ground.
Step:4 While in the air, quickly switch the position of your feet, landing with your opposite foot forward.
Step:5 Immediately lower your body back into a squat position and repeat the jump, switching your feet again.
Step:6 Continue alternating the position of your feet with each jump for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Tate Press
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, then rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing away from you.
Step:3 Press the dumbbells up until your arms are fully extended, then lower them back down to shoulder height.
Step:4 Rotate your wrists back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Wide Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Clean And Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the floor in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor, keeping it close to your body.
Step:4 As the barbell reaches your thighs, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the barbell up towards your chest.
Step:5 As the barbell reaches chest height, quickly drop under it and catch it at shoulder level, with your elbows pointing forward and your palms facing up.
Step:6 From the catch position, press the barbell overhead by extending your arms and pushing the barbell straight up.
Step:7 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Rope Seated Row
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the rowing machine with your feet flat on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the cable ropes with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Pull the cable ropes towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
Step:3 This will be your starting position.
Step:4 Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:5 Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Step:6 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:7 Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Rear Lunge
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Take a step backward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 Bend your left knee and lower your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side, stepping back with your left foot.

name: Dumbbell Standing Alternate Raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Raise one dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Step:4 Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:7 Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Lying Preacher Curl
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your arms extended over the edge, holding a barbell with an underhand grip.
Step:2 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Step:3 Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bent Over Row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, until your torso is almost parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Chin-Up
instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing towards you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Step:3 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Lunge
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your right knee until your thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Incline Fly
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Step:2 Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Band Front Raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band in front of your thighs with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and slowly raise them forward until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Crunch Floor
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Step:3 Engage your abs and lift your shoulders off the ground, curling forward towards your knees.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Straight Back Seated Row
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Step:4 Pull the cable handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to chest level.
Step:2 Lie flat on the bench with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Step:3 Grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the bar and lower it towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the bar touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the bar back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Band Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, with the band placed just above your knees.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, push your hips back and bend your knees to lower into a squat position.
Step:3 Make sure your knees are tracking over your toes and your weight is in your heels.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Rope Lying On Floor Tricep Extension
instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a cable machine and set it to the lowest position.
Step:2 Lie on the floor facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Step:3 Hold the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Step:4 Keep your upper arms stationary and slowly lower the rope towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Smith Deadlift
instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:3 Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight and chest up, and grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Engage your core and lift the bar by extending your hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body.
Step:5 Stand up straight, fully extending your hips and knees.
Step:6 Lower the bar back down by bending at the hips and knees, maintaining control and keeping your back straight.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold the kettlebell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, pushing your butt back.
Step:4 Swing the kettlebell back between your legs, keeping your arms straight and maintaining a flat back.
Step:5 Drive your hips forward and swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height, using the momentum generated by your hips.
Step:6 Allow the kettlebell to swing back down between your legs and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing towards your feet.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbells towards your chest, allowing your elbows to flare out to the sides.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Standing Palms In Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing inwards.
Step:2 Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, press the dumbbells upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell One Leg Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Lift one foot off the ground and extend it forward, keeping it parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Bend your standing leg and lower your body down as if sitting back into a chair, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Step:4 Lower yourself until your thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

name: Kettlebell Arnold Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing towards you.
Step:2 Engage your core and press the kettlebells overhead, rotating your palms to face forward as you extend your arms.
Step:3 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the kettlebells back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Standing Kickback
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Side Bend
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handle with one hand.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Slowly bend sideways at the waist, lowering the cable handle towards your knee.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position. Focus on smooth movement.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side.
Step:6 Alternate sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Seated One Leg Calf Raise
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Place one foot on a raised surface, such as a step or block, with your heel hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell with a hammer grip, meaning your palms are facing each other and your fingers are wrapped around the handle.
Step:4 Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, slowly raise your heel as high as possible by pushing through the ball of your foot.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Step:6 (deep variation) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.

name: Pull-In (On Stability Ball)
instructions: Step:1 Start by sitting on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Place your hands on the sides of the stability ball for support.
Step:3 Engage your abs and slowly roll your hips forward, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly extend your legs back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Scapular Pull-Up
instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Retract your shoulder blades by pulling them down and back.
Step:3 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Flutter Kicks
instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your hands by your sides.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground about 6 inches.
Step:3 Keeping your legs straight, alternate lifting one leg slightly higher than the other.
Step:4 Continue this fluttering motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: EZ Barbell Close-Grip Curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Step:3 Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Rear Lunge
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Step:2 Take a step backward with your right foot, landing on the ball of your foot.
Step:3 Bend both knees to lower your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other leg.

name: Cable Incline Bench Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the bench to a 45-degree incline.
Step:2 Attach the cable handles to the high pulleys.
Step:3 Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:4 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them to shoulder height.
Step:5 Push the handles forward and upward until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Reverse-Grip Pushdown
instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Using your triceps, push the bar down until your arms are fully extended and your triceps are contracted.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Seated One Arm Kickback
instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Bend your torso forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight back, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: Barbell Full Zercher Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the barbell for stability.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Step:4 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:5 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:6 Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Single Smith Shoulder Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 (single variation) Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the bar off the rack and position it at shoulder level, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:4 Press the bar upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to shoulder level.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Standing Concentration Curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Step:2 Rest your opposite hand on your thigh for support.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the weight up towards your shoulder.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly lower the weight back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

name: Band Pull Through
instructions: Step:1 Attach a resistance band to a sturdy anchor point at ground level.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Step forward to create tension in the band, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Hinge at the hips and push your glutes back, maintaining a slight bend in your knees.
Step:5 Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:6 Drive your hips forward and squeeze your glutes to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Glute-Ham Raise
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the glute-ham raise machine to fit your body.
Step:2 Position yourself face down on the machine with your ankles secured.
Step:3 Place your hands on your chest or cross them over your chest.
Step:4 Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift your upper body up towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Continue lifting until your body is in a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Side Split Squat
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, bending at the knees and hips.
Step:4 As you lower, push your knees out to the sides and keep your weight on your heels.
Step:5 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Kickback
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Bring your upper arms close to your sides, with your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Push-Up Medicine Ball
instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands on the medicine ball, shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Bench Squat
instructions: Step:1 Set up a barbell on a squat rack at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the rack, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Lift the barbell off the rack and step back, ensuring your feet are still shoulder-width apart.
Step:6 Lower your body down into a squat, keeping your knees in line with your toes.
Step:7 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Lever Standing Chest Press
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at chest level.
Step:3 Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back towards your chest, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Barbell Standing Close Grip Military Press
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly closer than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Overhead Curl
instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Step:5 Exhale and curl the bar down towards your forehead, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Step:7 Inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Captains Chair Straight Leg Raise
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the captain's chair with your back against the backrest and your forearms resting on the arm pads.
Step:2 Keep your upper body stable and your back straight.
Step:3 Engage your abs and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight.
Step:4 Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or as high as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable One Arm Lateral Raise
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with one hand, palm facing down, and stand far enough away from the machine so that there is tension on the cable.
Step:3 Keep your arm straight and slowly raise it out to the side until it is parallel to the ground.
